resources.string: Index a folder from the web app or upload your first photos.
"%1$s" will be deleted. The photos inside the album are kept in your library.
You will lose access to "%1$s". The owner can invite you back.
Add photos to this album from the asset viewer.
Group your memories into collections so they're easier to find.
No albums have been shared with you yet.
You don't have any albums with an active public link.
You don\'t have any albums yet.
You don\'t have permission to manage this album.
No share links yet. Anyone with the link will be able to view this album.
You haven't shared any links yet
The link will stop working and anyone who has it will no longer be able to view the content.
Only you have access. Invite people to give them read or write access.
Coming soon: settings, storage management, folder shortcuts and more.
Write the date into the original file (EXIF)
Writes EXIF into images and sets the file\'s modified date on every format, videos included.
Read-only external library: the file can\'t be modified.
"%1$s" will be moved to the trash. You can restore it from the web app.
Index a folder from the web app to see it here.
No folders have been shared with you yet.
It will live under /assets/shared/ and stay there even if your account is removed.
"%1$s" will be deleted. Photos stay on disk but lose their index entry; you can re-index from the web app.
Only you have access. Invite people to grant them read or write access.
No folders available as a target.
Type a term or apply filters to see results.
Archive a photo to keep it out of the timeline without removing it from the library.
All archived items will be brought back to the timeline.
Photos you move to the trash will appear here until you restore them or delete them forever.
All trashed items will be moved back to their original folder.
This cannot be undone. Every photo in the trash will be deleted permanently.
%1$d items will be deleted permanently. You won't be able to recover them.
Tap the heart on a photo from the viewer to see it here.
Tap to pick files from your device
%1$d uploaded · %2$d already in library · %3$d failed · %4$d pending
Photos tagged with GPS coordinates will show up here.
Once the server has finished clustering faces they'll show up here.
No suggested faces for this person.
No other people to merge with.
The server hasn't detected any faces in this photo.
Open the device share sheet (other apps, messaging, …).
Bundles the selection into an album and returns a public URL.
Anyone with this link will be able to see the photos.
Edit your profile, password, appearance and review storage usage.
Light, dark or follow the system
Manage users, review usage and clean up the server trash.
Retention and cleanup of expired items
Leave blank to use the default value.
Set a new password for "%1$s". The user will need to sign in with the new password.
"%1$s" will be permanently deleted. Their photos and associated data will be removed.

If you transfer the primary administrator role to "%1$s", you will lose the protection that prevents your account from being deleted, deactivated or demoted. Only the new primary administrator can revert this.
Your installation is up to date.
"%1$s" will be removed. Files on disk are kept but lose their index entry.
Only you can access this library.
Default for manual backfill runs from System. Each run can override it.
%1$d assets missing at least one AI pass
Master switch. When off, no photo is processed by the model.
Faces with a lower score are dropped. Range 0.0 – 1.0.
Maximum cosine distance to consider two faces the same person. Typical: 0.40 – 0.45.
Faces in [clustering threshold, this value) are recorded as suggestions. Must be ≥ clustering threshold.
Above this orphan count, pgvector kNN replaces the in-memory O(n²) pairwise loop.
Top-k queried per face in the kNN path.
Use the Large (1280 px) thumbnail instead of the original. Reduces I/O. ArcFace crops to 112x112 anyway.
Master switch. When off, no photo is processed for objects.
Objects with a lower score are dropped. Range 0.0 – 1.0.
Minimum fraction of the image side (0.02 = 2 %). Filters tiny objects out.
Upper cap. Objects with the highest score win.
Master switch. When off, no scenes are classified on any photo.
Rank 1 (best guess) is always kept. Lower ranks only if they exceed this score.
Upper cap. Stored in ranking order.
Master switch. When off, no text is extracted from any photo.
Lines with lower confidence are dropped. Range 0.0 – 1.0.
Upper cap per image. Useful for dense documents.
Master switch. When off, no embeddings are generated and content search is unavailable.
Stable identifier of the CLIP model. Default: mclip-vit-b32-v1.
⚠ Changing the model version invalidates every existing embedding. Until you run a backfill with "Only missing" OFF, semantic search will mix old and new vectors and results will be incoherent.
Upper bound for semantic search results. Lower = stricter results. Typical range: 0.5 – 0.9.
Where this task runs. "GPU" requires the CUDA ML image; if unavailable it falls back to CPU automatically.

⚠ OCR on GPU reserves a lot of video memory (an exhaustive, non-tunable convolution search). On a GPU shared with other tasks it can exhaust VRAM and fail; if you see "out of memory" errors, keep it on CPU.
Stored only on this device. The local URL is used automatically when reachable (same WiFi as the server).
Connected via the public URL (no local URL configured)
Local URL did not respond from this network.
Restore the capture date from EXIF
Re-pair Live Photo stills and clips
Defaults to the global value (%1$d). Editable only for this run.
No pending photos — everything is processed.
Everything pending is already in the queue. The ML engine will process it in the background; nothing new to enqueue.
Queued %1$d. The ML engine will process them in the background.
Stopping only halts new enqueues; jobs already in the queue keep being processed in the background.
Removes thumbnail files that no longer match any asset.
Scans indexed assets and marks those no longer on disk as missing.
Re-reads file sizes from disk to fix quota counters.
Permanently deletes every item in every user's trash.
Deletes from the database the assets marked as missing and the folders whose directory no longer exists on disk.
Export the selected database records. The backup does not include media files; only metadata and structure.
Media files (photos and videos) are not included in the backup. You must manage them separately.
Server settings, users, folders (structure), external libraries (definitions) and permissions. Ideal for migrating the install to another machine without the current library.
Everything above + albums, assets, EXIF, thumbnails, tags and shared links. No ML data — it will be reprocessed on restore.
Everything above + faces, people, CLIP embeddings, OCR, objects and scenes. Noticeably larger file.
Backup restore is only available from the web app for now.
Re-reads EXIF, GPS and other fields directly from the files.
Infer dates from name or folder
Write the date into the file (EXIF)
Pick a folder from your device and you'll see every photo and video inside it. Then sync the ones you want into your account.
No photos or videos in this folder.
Device backup is not available on this platform yet.
%1$d already-synced files will be removed from the device. This cannot be undone.
Video playback is not available on this platform
Run a state check to see how many are missing
Turn backup on to start uploading your photos.
%1$d file(s) could not be uploaded. Open Photonne to review them.
No additional details from the server.
Upload in the background without opening the app
Uploads more files at once. Faster on Wi-Fi, but uses more battery and data
%1$d items will be moved to your trash. You can restore them from the web app.
Alerts about your library will show up here.
Paste it into a message and send it to your admin.
Every file found in your storage has a supported type.
File type not supported by the app.
